spec_trace.roadmap_pointer: Handshake_Master_Spec v02.127 Dev Command Center (DCC) MVP, frontend shell slice

companion_intent_doc.role: Operator/no-context-model handoff anchor; survives .GOV abandonment because it lives in the product tree.

draft_scope.intent:
- What: Deliver the Dev Command Center (DCC) UI shell - the canonical control surface that binds Locus work (WP/MT) <-> workspaces (worktrees) <-> sessions <-> approvals/logs/diffs, without bypassing Workflow Engine, gates, or Flight Recorder.
- Why: Make governed development usable (approvals as inbox, VCS as panel, predictable traceable operations) while enabling safe parallel work and deterministic recovery/handoffs.
- Backend-first rule: this UI WP projects over the DCC control-plane backend (WP-1-Dev-Command-Center-Control-Plane-Backend-v1). DCC is not authority; it is a projection and steering surface over backend artifacts.
draft_scope.scope_sketch:
- IN_SCOPE (already committed on feat/WP-1-Dev-Command-Center-UI-Shell-v1 @ 585e42ad):
  - app/src/App.tsx module-tab + project-tab + pane/splitter shell
  - app/src/App.css pane/splitter/lab grid styles
  - app/src/App.test.tsx frontend shell tests
  - app/scripts/handshake-screenshot-capture.mjs --backend-fixture mode, renderer-error trap, capture dimensions, quiet automation metadata
  - app/scripts/handshake-screenshot-capture.test.mjs fixture mode tests
- UI surfaces introduced:
  - Module tabs: MAIN, CKC, INGEST, STAGE, LAB, STUDIO
  - Project tabs: HANDSHAKE, FAST-FOCUS, BUURTMAN, CASTKIT
  - Pane primitives with stable-id taxonomy main-window.pane.{id}.*
  - Splitter primitives with stable-id taxonomy main-window.splitter.{id}
  - Activity-bar items with stable-id taxonomy main-window.activity.{id}
  - Capture backend fixture endpoints under http://127.0.0.1:37501/*
- OUT_OF_SCOPE:
  - Anything under src/backend/handshake_core/** (route to a backend WP)
  - Calendar sync logic, calendar storage, calendar capability routing (see WP-1-Calendar-Sync-Engine-v2)
  - Software-delivery projection-discipline runtime contracts (see WP-1-Software-Delivery-Projection-Surface-Discipline-v1)
  - DCC control-plane backend (see WP-1-Dev-Command-Center-Control-Plane-Backend-v1)
  - Repo-governance protocol changes; this WP is intentionally outside the heavy .GOV/ packet ceremony
  - GitHub PR/comment sync, multi-user shared approvals, full external conversation ingestion
draft_scope.acceptance_criteria:
- Operator can launch the new app shell, see module + project tabs, switch panes and split ratios with keyboard and pointer, and capture a visual-debug screenshot via the fixture-backed playwright path without a running Rust backend.
- Frontend tests for App and handshake-screenshot-capture pass locally.
- Branch merges cleanly to main with no Rust backend, .GOV/, or other-WP scope contamination.
draft_scope.risks_unknowns:
- Risk: stable-id taxonomy may drift before a layout-projection registry exists, breaking later DCC pane wiring. Mitigation: keep the main-window.* prefix stable and document any rename in the intent doc.
- Risk: backend fixture diverges from real /api/kernel/dcc_projection contract once the backend WP lands. Mitigation: fixture is auto-disabled when a real backend responds on http://127.0.0.1:37501/health.
- Risk: under kernel-reset stance there is no signed scope, so scope creep is not deterministically blocked. Mitigation: this stub + the companion intent doc are the soft scope record; any out-of-scope edit should produce a separate WP or stub.
draft_scope.governance_artifact_stance: Authored under operator-declared kernel-reset stance (2026-05-27) that retires the heavy .GOV/ packet/refinement/MT chain in favor of product-tree intent docs plus minimal stubs. No refinement, no signature, no packet. Stub + intent doc are the soft scope record until the new product-based governance lands.

assumptions_to_attack: A stub is planning metadata, not execution authority.
Under the kernel-reset stance there is no signed scope; the intent doc + stub are the soft scope record only.
minimum_controls: Do not treat absence of red-team or signature as license for scope creep; out-of-scope edits go in a new WP or stub.
Keep stable-id main-window.* taxonomy stable for downstream DCC pane wiring.
